title: United States and Iran intensify mutual attacks as mediator proposes a 10-day ceasefire
summary: The conflict leaves US soldiers dead and Iranian attacks in Jordan and Kuwait, amid a ceasefire offered by a mediating country.

title: China and Russia conduct live-fire exercises in Japan's exclusive economic zone
summary: The Japanese Defense Minister denounces the violation of its EEZ and warns of the strengthening of Sino-Russian military cooperation.

title: Spain defeats Argentina in extra time and wins the World Cup for the second time
summary: The Spanish team prevails with a goal from Torres and an outstanding performance by Yamal, while Messi bids farewell in tears and Mbappé is crowned as top scorer.

title: Houthis declare a maritime blockade against Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea
summary: The Iran-backed armed group threatens shipping routes in the strategic Red Sea.

title: Hamas elects Khalil al-Hayya as new supreme leader
summary: Al-Hayya, who led the ceasefire negotiations in Gaza, assumes leadership of the Islamist group.

title: Toyota president reaffirms commitment to domestic production and innovation
summary: The company plans to maintain its supply chain in Japan and improve efficiency with artificial intelligence and robotics at its new plant in Aichi.

title: EU imposes record fine of over 100 billion yen on Alibaba and AliExpress
summary: The e-commerce platforms are sanctioned for allowing the sale of illegal products.

title: Japan suffers heatwave with temperatures over 40 degrees and dozens hospitalized
summary: The end of the rainy season gives way to extreme heat causing heatstroke and deaths.

title: Donald Trump sparks controversy at World Cup final with boos and controversial gestures
summary: The former US president is booed during the award ceremony and has a controversial exchange with the Spanish prime minister.

title: Katsuya Kakunaka, historic Lotte Marines batter, announces his retirement
summary: The first batting champion from an independent league ends a 20-year career in Japanese professional baseball.

title: Andy Burnham assumes office as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
summary: The Labour leader becomes the seventh British leader in a decade, promising results in his administration.

title: Spain tops FIFA rankings after winning the World Cup; Japan rises to 17th place
summary: The post-tournament update reflects the rise of the most outstanding teams.

title: Woman seriously injured after being hit by a jet ski on a Hyogo beach
summary: The accident occurred on the coast of Ako when the victim was struck by a watercraft driven by an acquaintance.

title: Princess Kiko attends charity concert for patients with neuromyelitis optica
summary: The Japanese princess showed her support for patients with this neurological disease in a moving event.

title: ASEAN foreign ministers begin meeting to discuss disputes in the South China Sea
summary: The meeting also raises expectations about possible contacts between Wang Yi and the foreign ministers of Japan and the United States.

title: China assaults a Filipino soldier with a stick in the South China Sea
summary: The incident occurred at Ayungin Reef, controlled by the Philippines, and has been condemned by both sides.

title: Search begins for remains of Japanese soldiers who fell in Taiwan during World War II
summary: Half a century later, a research team begins excavations on the southern coast of Taiwan.

title: Fire at Japan's Self-Defense Forces Asaka base
summary: The fire, which originated in the garbage area, was extinguished after an hour without causing injuries.

title: Thousands protest in India against Education Minister called by the 'Cockroach People's Party'
summary: The mobilization demands the resignation of the Education Minister and has led to meetings with leaders of the ruling party.

title: Yoshinobu Yamamoto reaches ten wins for second consecutive season
summary: The Orix Buffaloes pitcher throws a complete game without walks to achieve his tenth victory.

title: Okinawa Shogaku and Shirakaba Gakuen qualify for the national Koshien baseball tournament
summary: Defending champion Okinawa Shogaku achieves its second consecutive qualification, while Shirakaba Gakuen returns after two years.

title: Nagoya sumo tournament records weakness of yokozuna and advance of Shishi
summary: Wrestler Shishi secures his majority victory while the grand champions accumulate losses.

title: Japan, UK, and Italy push development of next-generation fighter jet
summary: The Japanese Defense Minister describes the project as a model of security cooperation after visiting the full-scale mockup in the UK.
